today.name: CREOSOTE
today.nextAction: v0.4.11 — BACKLIT UNIFORM + READABLE: fixes the two issues seen in v0.4.10 in the DAW. (1) TEXT WAS BLURRY: EB::glowText drew the glyphs themselves at ring offsets, which fattened/smeared them. It now renders the text once, gaussian-blurs it into a soft halo (cached per text/size/colour so the AMBER heat overlay doesn't re-blur each frame), composites that halo, then draws the CRISP text on top — a true text-shadow, sharp and readable. (2) TILES WEREN'T UNIFORM: the cap bloom was sized off each tile's WIDTH, so wide selectors read bright and even while narrow value tiles were centre-hot/edge-dark. EB::backlitCap is now a SUBTLE, EVEN, low-contrast warm panel (bloom peak alpha ~0.31 -> ~0.13) that reads the same at any tile width; the 'lit' character now comes from the glowing TEXT, as in the mockup. Still paint-only (no DSP, no layout). VERIFY IN ABLETON: legends are sharp and readable with a soft glow; every tile (wide selector or narrow value) reads uniformly lit; still zero blue (COMPRESS caps amber, aqua only in strip+rail). KNOWN (unchanged): GRIT selectors are historically EC::gold while its value tiles are EC::coral, so GRIT's lean isn't perfectly uniform yet — a small .cpp accent pass, deferred. Remaining UI punch-list unchanged: GRIT deep controls -> TWEAK, AMBER vertical stage-tags, top-down GR meter, raise bottom section.
